title: galium aparine var. intermedium
intro: Galium aparine var. intermedium, commonly known as cleavers, is a type of flowering plant in the bedstraw family (Galium). It is a perennial herb with a sprawling habit, often growing in dense clusters, and is recognized by its sticky, hairy stems and leaves. Cleavers are valued for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and have been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, such as skin conditions, respiratory problems, and digestive issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and support the immune system.
